metadata.description: Task breakdown for implementing workout creation flow with custom exercises, intelligent search, template management, and daily workouts to achieve 70% new user engagement and <10 minute first workout completion

tasks.title: Create CustomExercise database entity and DAO
tasks.description: Implement Room entity for user-created custom exercises with full CRUD operations and user-scoped data isolation

implementation_details.approach: Extend existing entity patterns with user-scoped custom exercise storage
integration_points: User authentication context for data isolation

acceptance_criteria: CustomExercise entity supports name, muscle groups, equipment, difficulty
All operations scoped to authenticated user ID
Name uniqueness enforced per user with max 100 characters
DAO methods return Flow for reactive updates
validation_steps: Unit tests pass for all DAO operations
User data isolation verified in tests
example_code: @Entity(tableName = "custom_exercises") data class CustomExerciseEntity(@PrimaryKey val id: String, @ColumnInfo(name = "user_id") val userId: String, val name: String, val primaryMuscleGroup: String, val equipment: String, val secondaryMuscles: List<String>?, val difficulty: Int?, val notes: String?, val createdAt: Instant, val updatedAt: Instant)

tasks.title: Create WorkoutTemplate entity distinct from daily workouts
tasks.description: Implement separate entities for reusable workout templates vs daily workout instances to support template reuse patterns

implementation_details.approach: Create separate template and daily workout storage with foreign key relationships

acceptance_criteria: Templates preserve exercise order and target sets/reps/weight
Template names unique per user with 100 character limit
Daily workouts reference templates but store actual performance data
Support template editing without affecting historical workouts
validation_steps: Data integrity maintained across template modifications
example_code: @Entity(tableName = "workout_templates") data class WorkoutTemplateEntity(@PrimaryKey val id: String, val userId: String, val name: String, val exercises: List<String>, val createdAt: Instant, val updatedAt: Instant)

tasks.title: Create exercise library seed data with 50+ exercises
tasks.description: Populate database with comprehensive exercise library covering all major muscle groups with equipment categorization

implementation_details.approach: Create JSON seed data with 50+ exercises across all muscle groups and equipment types

acceptance_criteria: 50+ exercises covering Chest, Back, Shoulders, Arms, Legs, Core, Cardio
Multiple equipment variations for common movements (e.g., lateral raises with bands, dumbbells)
Proper muscle group and equipment categorization
Exercise names support fuzzy search patterns
validation_steps: All muscle groups have adequate exercise coverage
Exercise variations properly grouped by movement pattern
Seed data loads successfully on app initialization
example_code: val exercises = listOf(ExerciseLibraryEntity("chest-bench-press-barbell", "Bench Press", "Chest", "BARBELL", listOf("Chest", "Shoulders", "Triceps")), ExerciseLibraryEntity("chest-bench-press-dumbbell", "Dumbbell Bench Press", "Chest", "DUMBBELLS", listOf("Chest", "Shoulders", "Triceps")))

tasks.title: Create CustomExercise domain model
tasks.description: Implement domain model for user-created exercises with validation and business rules

implementation_details.approach: Create immutable domain model with validation and business rules
functions_to_implement: CustomExercise.create(name: String, primaryMuscle: ExerciseCategory, equipment: Equipment): CustomExercise

acceptance_criteria: Name validation (1-100 characters, non-blank)
Primary muscle group required from predefined categories
Equipment selection from existing Equipment enum
Optional secondary muscles, difficulty (1-10), notes (max 500 chars)
validation_steps: Validation rules enforced in model constructor
Business rules tested with edge cases
example_code: data class CustomExercise(val id: CustomExerciseId, val userId: String, val name: String, val primaryMuscle: ExerciseCategory, val equipment: Equipment, val secondaryMuscles: Set<ExerciseCategory> = emptySet(), val difficulty: Int? = null, val notes: String? = null, val createdAt: Instant, val updatedAt: Instant) { init { require(name.isNotBlank() && name.length <= 100) } }

tasks.title: Create WorkoutTemplate domain model
tasks.description: Implement domain model for reusable workout templates with exercise ordering and targeting

implementation_details.approach: Extend existing workout patterns for template/instance distinction

acceptance_criteria: Template names unique per user (max 100 characters)
Exercise order preservation with reordering support
Template-to-daily-workout conversion with data population
Template editing without affecting historical workouts
validation_steps: Template immutability with proper update methods
example_code: data class WorkoutTemplate(val id: WorkoutTemplateId, val userId: String, val name: String, val exercises: List<TemplateExercise>, val createdAt: Instant, val updatedAt: Instant) { fun createDailyWorkout(): DailyWorkout = DailyWorkout.fromTemplate(this) }

tasks.title: Implement CreateCustomExerciseUseCase
tasks.description: Create use case for custom exercise creation with validation and user-scoped storage

implementation_details.approach: Implement use case with input validation and repository interaction

acceptance_criteria: Name uniqueness validation per user
Input sanitization and validation
Proper error handling with user-friendly messages
User-scoped exercise creation
example_code: class CreateCustomExerciseUseCase @Inject constructor(private val repository: CustomExerciseRepository, private val authRepository: AuthRepository) { suspend operator fun invoke(input: CreateCustomExerciseInput): Result<CustomExercise> { val userId = authRepository.getCurrentUserId() ?: return Result.failure(AuthenticationException()) return repository.createCustomExercise(userId, input) } }

tasks.title: Implement SearchExercisesUseCase with variations
tasks.description: Create intelligent search that shows exercise variations based on equipment and movement patterns

implementation_details.approach: Implement fuzzy search with movement pattern matching and equipment filtering
libraries_needed: Apache Commons Text for fuzzy matching

acceptance_criteria: Search 'lateral raises' returns band and dumbbell variations
Fuzzy matching handles typos and partial matches
Results filtered by user's available equipment
Custom exercises included in search results
Response time <200ms for typical queries
validation_steps: Search accuracy tested with various queries
example_code: class SearchExercisesUseCase @Inject constructor(private val exerciseRepository: ExerciseLibraryRepository, private val customExerciseRepository: CustomExerciseRepository) { suspend fun search(query: String, userEquipment: Set<Equipment>): Flow<List<Exercise>> = combine(exerciseRepository.searchExercises(query), customExerciseRepository.searchCustomExercises(query)) { library, custom -> (library + custom).filter { exercise -> userEquipment.contains(exercise.equipment) }.sortedBy { fuzzyDistance(query, it.name) } } }

tasks.title: Implement CustomExerciseRepositoryImpl
tasks.description: Create repository implementation with Room local storage and Firestore cloud sync for custom exercises

implementation_details.approach: Local-first repository with background Firestore sync using existing patterns

acceptance_criteria: Local-first data access with offline support
Background sync to Firestore for user-scoped data
Conflict resolution for simultaneous edits
Proper error handling and retry logic
validation_steps: Repository operations work offline and online
example_code: @Singleton class CustomExerciseRepositoryImpl @Inject constructor(private val dao: CustomExerciseDao, private val firestore: FirebaseFirestore, private val mapper: CustomExerciseMapper) : CustomExerciseRepository { override suspend fun createCustomExercise(userId: String, input: CreateCustomExerciseInput): Result<CustomExercise> = try { val entity = mapper.toEntity(input, userId); dao.insert(entity); syncToFirestore(entity); Result.success(mapper.toDomain(entity)) } catch (e: Exception) { Result.failure(e) } }

tasks.title: Create CustomExerciseCreationScreen
tasks.description: Build Compose screen for custom exercise creation with form validation and user feedback

implementation_details.approach: Material 3 Compose screen with MVI pattern and form validation

acceptance_criteria: Form with required fields: name, primary muscle, equipment
Optional fields: secondary muscles, difficulty, notes
Real-time validation with inline error messages
Loading states during exercise creation
Success feedback and navigation after creation
validation_steps: UI states handled properly (loading, error, success)
example_code: @Composable fun CustomExerciseCreationScreen(viewModel: CustomExerciseCreationViewModel = hiltViewModel(), onNavigateBack: () -> Unit) { val uiState by viewModel.uiState.collectAsStateWithLifecycle(); Column { TopAppBar(title = { Text("Create Exercise") }, navigationIcon = { IconButton(onClick = onNavigateBack) { Icon(Icons.Default.ArrowBack, "Back") } }); CustomExerciseForm(state = uiState, onNameChanged = viewModel::updateName, onSubmit = viewModel::createExercise) } }

tasks.title: Enhance ExerciseSelectionScreen with variations
tasks.description: Update exercise selection to show equipment variations and integrate custom exercise creation

modify: app/src/main/java/com/example/liftrix/ui/workout/creation/ExerciseSelectionScreen.kt - Add variations display and custom exercise option

implementation_details.approach: Enhance existing exercise selection with variation grouping and custom exercise integration

acceptance_criteria: Search results grouped by movement pattern with equipment variations
Custom exercises displayed alongside library exercises
Add Custom Exercise button prominently placed
Visual indicators for custom vs library exercises
Maintains <200ms search response time
example_code: @Composable fun ExerciseVariationList(exerciseGroup: ExerciseGroup, onExerciseSelected: (Exercise) -> Unit) { Card { Column { Text(exerciseGroup.movementPattern, style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium); LazyRow { items(exerciseGroup.variations) { exercise -> ExerciseVariationCard(exercise = exercise, onClick = { onExerciseSelected(exercise) }) } } } } }

tasks.title: Add QuickWorkoutFab component
tasks.description: Implement floating action button for quick daily workout creation with template selection

implementation_details.approach: Material 3 FAB with modal bottom sheet for template selection

acceptance_criteria: FAB visible on main screens with proper Material 3 styling
Modal shows template options plus 'Create from Scratch'
Quick access to start workout without template creation
Proper navigation to workout execution screen
example_code: @Composable fun QuickWorkoutFab(onClick: () -> Unit, modifier: Modifier = Modifier) { FloatingActionButton(onClick = onClick, modifier = modifier, containerColor = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary) { Icon(Icons.Default.Add, contentDescription = "Quick Workout") } }

tasks.title: Implement workout template vs daily workout UI distinction
tasks.description: Create clear visual and navigation differences between template creation and daily workout execution

implementation_details.approach: Separate UI flows with distinct visual indicators and navigation paths

acceptance_criteria: Clear visual distinction between template and daily workout screens
Template creation shows 'Save as Template' messaging
Daily workout shows 'Start Workout' with immediate execution
Option to convert completed daily workout to template
Breadcrumb or title clearly indicates current mode
validation_steps: UI distinctions are clear and intuitive
example_code: @Composable fun QuickWorkoutScreen(templateId: String?, viewModel: DailyWorkoutViewModel = hiltViewModel()) { val uiState by viewModel.uiState.collectAsStateWithLifecycle(); Column { TopAppBar(title = { Text("Today's Workout") }); if (templateId != null) { Text("Starting from template", style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium) }; WorkoutExecutionInterface(uiState = uiState, onSetCompleted = viewModel::completeSet) } }

tasks.title: Integrate custom exercise creation end-to-end
tasks.description: Connect custom exercise creation from UI through to database storage with proper error handling

modify: app/src/main/java/com/example/liftrix/ui/workout/creation/ExerciseSelectionScreen.kt - Add navigation to custom exercise creation

implementation_details.approach: End-to-end integration testing and navigation wiring

acceptance_criteria: Custom exercise creation accessible from exercise selection
Created exercises immediately available in search results
Proper error handling with user feedback
Navigation flows work seamlessly
validation_steps: End-to-end flow tested with real data
example_code: // In ExerciseSelectionScreen LaunchedEffect(viewModel.customExerciseCreated) { customExercise -> if (customExercise != null) { viewModel.refreshExerciseList(); viewModel.clearCustomExerciseCreated() } }

tasks.title: Connect intelligent search to UI
tasks.description: Integrate search with variations functionality with exercise selection UI for seamless user experience

implementation_details.approach: Real-time search integration with debouncing and performance optimization

acceptance_criteria: Search results update in real-time with <200ms response
Exercise variations grouped and displayed correctly
Equipment filtering works seamlessly
No search performance degradation with large datasets
example_code: class ExerciseSelectionViewModel @Inject constructor(private val searchUseCase: SearchExercisesUseCase) { private val searchQuery = MutableStateFlow(""); val searchResults = searchQuery.debounce(300).flatMapLatest { query -> if (query.isBlank()) flowOf(emptyList()) else searchUseCase.search(query, userEquipment) }.stateIn(viewModelScope, SharingStarted.WhileSubscribed(5000), emptyList()) }

tasks.title: Write unit tests for custom exercise use cases
tasks.description: Comprehensive unit testing for custom exercise creation, validation, and search functionality

implementation_details.approach: MockK-based unit tests following existing test patterns with comprehensive coverage

acceptance_criteria: 90%+ code coverage for use cases
All validation scenarios tested
Search performance and accuracy validated
Error handling paths covered
example_code: @ExtendWith(MockKExtension::class) class CreateCustomExerciseUseCaseTest { @MockK private lateinit var repository: CustomExerciseRepository; @MockK private lateinit var authRepository: AuthRepository; @Test fun `should create exercise with valid input`() = runTest { every { authRepository.getCurrentUserId() } returns "user123"; coEvery { repository.createCustomExercise(any(), any()) } returns Result.success(mockCustomExercise); val result = useCase(validInput); assertThat(result.isSuccess).isTrue() } }

tasks.title: Write UI tests for exercise selection and creation
tasks.description: Compose UI tests for exercise selection screen, custom exercise creation, and workout building flows

implementation_details.approach: Compose testing with Hilt test modules and realistic user interaction scenarios

acceptance_criteria: Critical user paths tested end-to-end
Form validation UI behavior verified
Search and selection interactions work correctly
Accessibility features tested
validation_steps: UI tests pass on different device configurations
Performance requirements met in test environment
example_code: @HiltAndroidTest class ExerciseSelectionScreenTest { @get:Rule val hiltRule = HiltAndroidRule(this); @get:Rule val composeTestRule = createAndroidComposeRule<MainActivity>(); @Test fun should_display_search_results() { composeTestRule.setContent { ExerciseSelectionScreen(onExerciseSelected = {}) }; composeTestRule.onNodeWithText("Search exercises").performTextInput("lateral raises"); composeTestRule.onNodeWithText("Dumbbell Lateral Raises").assertIsDisplayed() } }
